mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding labor income and aggregate demand, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations revise upward.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in PAYEMS coincide with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of instances over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate below 0.25 percent reflects a zero lower bound environment where conventional monetary policy is exhausted and economic slack remains severe, causing equity risk premiums to expand and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods with FEDFUNDS below 0.25 percent produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55 percent of triggered months, or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and increases in the unemployment rate generate a demand-labor double drag that reduces corporate revenue visibility and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint realizations of falling RSAFS and rising UNRATE fail to predict negative 20-day returns in at least 60 percent of occurrences, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant at the 5 percent level.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days only when the unemployment rate is elevated above 7 percent, reflecting supply-led recovery dynamics in a post-shock labor slack environment.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 7.0 percent, month-over-month increases in INDPRO coincide with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45 percent of instances, or the conditional Sharpe ratio is not positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales signals robust consumer spending momentum that translates into immediate earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in RSAFS are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50 percent of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month annualized inflation rate above 3.5 percent as measured by CPIAUCSL triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days because rising input costs compress corporate margins and increase the probability of future monetary tightening.
failure_condition: Triggered instances of CPIAUCSL growth above 3.5 percent annualized produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55 percent of occurrences, or the mean forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls and month-over-month growth in industrial production captures coincident labor-market and supply-chain expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS and INDPRO fails to achieve a t-statistic greater than 2.0 in predicting 60-day returns, or the directional accuracy is below 55 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 550 thousand units signal a stalled construction sector and weak household formation, which reduces cyclical earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances with HOUST below 550 thousand produce positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55 percent of occurrences, or the mean forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).
